# No3d Tools — Complete Reference

> This document contains full documentation for every No3d Tools product.
> Use it as context when helping a user with No3d Tools assets in Blender.

Website: https://no3dtools.com
Subscribe: https://no3dtools.com/subscribe.html
Account: https://no3dtools.com/account.html
Getting Started: https://no3dtools.com/guide.html

---

# Product Catalog

## Airpods Max

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: EMPTY | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset | Handle: airpods-max

# Airpods Max

## Description

Detailed description of the Airpods Max Blender asset.

## Features

- **Asset Type**: Object
- **Blender Version**: 5.0.0+
- **File Size**: 0.11 MB
- **Catalog**: All Catalogs

## Usage Instructions

### Basic Usage

1. Download the `.blend` file
2. Open in Blender 5.0.0+
3. Import or drag the asset into your scene
4. Use the asset as needed

### Advanced Usage

- Customize the asset parameters
- Combine with other assets
- Use in your own projects

## Technical Details

- **Blender Version**: 5.0.0+
- **Asset Type**: Object
- **File Size**: 0.11 MB
- **Dependencies**: None

## Examples

This asset can be used for:
- 3D modeling projects
- Animation workflows
- Game development
- Architectural visualization

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Commercial use license included.

## Apple Watch Charger

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: OBJECT | Blender: 5.0+ | Tags: blender, geometry-nodes, object, accessory | Handle: apple-watch-charger

# Apple Watch Charger

## Description

A 3D printable Apple Watch charger cable holder/stand. This is a static mesh asset with a thin modifier wrapper -- the main geometry is pre-modeled and ready to print. Extremely useful to use as a quick reference for creating Apple watch accessories

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ APPLE WATCH CHARGER                  │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                           Geometry   ○
│                                      │
○ Geometry                             │
□ Apple Watch                          │
□ Bool Object                          │
▮ Socket                               │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Apple Watch Charger** from the Asset Browser into your scene
2. Toggle **Apple Watch** to show/hide the watch model preview
3. Toggle **Bool Object** to show/hide the boolean cutting geometry
4. **Socket** adjusts the cable socket sizing
5. Export as STL for 3D printing

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Arrow

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: OBJECT | Blender: 5.0+ | Tags: blender, geometry-nodes, mesh-tool | Handle: arrow

# Arrow

## Description

An arrow generator that creates 3D arrow geometry from a mesh line. The modifier is a thin wrapper -- the geometry is generated internally from the input mesh line with no user-facing parameters beyond the input geometry.

Static mesh asset -- no user-facing parameters. Drag into your scene and use as-is or modify the underlying mesh line to change the arrow shape.

## Usage

1. Drag **Arrow** from the Asset Browser into your scene
2. The arrow geometry is generated from the underlying mesh line
3. Edit the mesh line in Edit Mode to change the arrow path and shape

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Basic Skadis Panel 200mm

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: OBJECT | Blender: 5.0+ | Tags: blender, 3d-printing, skadis, ikea, pegboard, organizer | Handle: basic-skadis-panel-200mm

# Basic Skadis Panel 200mm

## Description

A standard IKEA Skadis pegboard panel (200mm) modeled for 3D printing. Precise dimensions matching the real Skadis hole pattern. Drop into your scene as a reference or export directly for printing custom Skadis-compatible panels.

Static mesh asset — no configurable parameters. Drag and drop to use.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

## Bubble Putty Generator

Procedural bubble putty generator that wraps mesh objects in organic bubble forms

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ BUBBLE PUTTY GENERATOR                       │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                              │

□ MESH OBJECTS                                 │
▮ Soften                                       │
▮ Max bubble size                              │
◉ Filament Color                               │
▮ Resoliution                                  │
▮ Putty Factor                                 │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Chrome Crayon

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: GEOMETRY | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: 3D Printable, Mograph | Handle: chrome-crayon

Chrome Crayon is a node-powered Blender utility that lets you turn simple hand-drawn curves into amazing cyber sigil 3D geometry. Effortlessly turn sketches into intricate, futuristic forms that are fully 3D printable--while also being light and fast enough to use in sophisticated digital art, animation, and motion graphics.

Harnessing the flexibility of geometry nodes, Chrome Crayon allows you to generate high-detail custom geometry with just a few clicks. Draw your curves directly in Blender or import vector paths, add the Chrome Crayon node group, and immediately see your designs transformed into clean, striking 3D meshes.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ CHROME CRAYON 3D V4.3                    │
├──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                               Geometry   ○
│                                          │
○ Geometry                                 │
□ Show Pt Count                            │
□ crv preview                              │
▮ Line Thiccness          ⌖               │
▮ Peak Height             ⌖               │
▮ resolution                               │
▮ Sigilize                                 │
▮ Soften                                   │
◆ 3D material                              │
□ FLATTEN                                  │
◉ Flat Color                               │
◆ Material                                 │
▮ Extrude Base            ⌖               │
▮ SPIRO                                    │
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Draw a curve in Blender (Bezier, Pencil, or convert Grease Pencil/mesh edges to a curve)
2. Drag **Chrome Crayon** from the Asset Browser onto your curve object
3. **Line Thiccness** controls the stroke width
4. **Peak Height** sets the maximum extrusion height of the chrome ridges
5. **resolution** adjusts the mesh density of the generated stroke
6. **Sigilize** applies a sigil-style simplification to the curve (higher = more simplified)
7. **Soften** smooths sharp peaks in the chrome extrusion
8. Assign a **3D material** for the chrome shading in 3D mode
9. Toggle **FLATTEN** to collapse the stroke to a flat 2D plane
10. **Flat Color** sets the emission color when flattened
11. **Extrude Base** adds a solid base beneath the chrome stroke
12. **SPIRO** applies a spirograph transformation to the curve path
13. Toggle **Show Pt Count** or **crv preview** for debugging curve point info

## Changelog

### Version 5.0
- Initial release: node system for curve-to-cyber geometry
- Optimized for both 3D printing and digital scenes

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

### Usage Guide

# Chrome Crayon - Asset User Guide

## Getting Started

### Installation

1. **Download the Asset**
   - Locate the Chrome Crayon `.blend` file in your downloads or asset library.
   - Ensure you have Blender 5.0.0 or later installed.

### Adding Chrome Crayon to a New Asset Library

To create a dedicated library for NO3D assets:

1. **Create a New Asset Library Folder**
   - In your file system, create a new folder and name it **no3d tools library** (or a name you'll recognize).
2. **Add Chrome Crayon**
   - Copy `Chrome Crayon.blend` into your new **no3d tools library** folder.

3. **Register the Library in Blender**
   - In Blender, go to **Edit > Preferences > File Paths > Asset Libraries**.
   - Click **Add**, then select your new **no3d tools library** folder. Click **Accept** and close Preferences.

Now, in Blender’s Asset Browser, choose **no3d tools library** from the Asset Libraries dropdown. Your Chrome Crayon assets are ready to use—just drag them into your project!

---

### Adding Chrome Crayon to an Existing Asset Library

If you already have an asset library folder (for example, **no3d tools library**):

1. Copy `Chrome Crayon.blend` into your existing **no3d tools library** folder.
2. Open `Chrome Crayon.blend` in Blender.
3. Mark your desired assets (**Mark as Asset**), and save the file.
4. In Blender’s Asset Browser, make sure your asset library is selected. Chrome Crayon assets will now appear—drag them into your project whenever you need them!

That's it!

---

## Using the Chrome Crayon Asset

Drag-and-Drop from Asset Browser

1. **Open Asset Browser**
   - Go to the Asset Browser panel in Blender (accessible in the main window, or via Area change).

2. **Find Chrome Crayon**
   - Look in the section or category (such as Materials, Node Groups, or Objects) where you saved the Chrome Crayon asset.

3. **Use the Asset**
   - For materials: drag and drop Chrome Crayon onto your selected object in the 3D viewport.
   - For node groups: drag directly into the Shader Editor.
   - For objects: drag into your scene collection.

---

## Customizing the Chrome Crayon Asset

Chrome Crayon assets are fully customizable.

- To customize parameters, look in the **Modifier panel** when the node group is added as a modifier—there you'll find all adjustable settings.
- OR use the included **custom gizmo interface** that appears in the 3D Viewport when the asset is selected, allowing interactive adjustment of key parameters.

### For Objects

- Imported Chrome Crayon objects come fully set up with the material applied. You can duplicate, modify, or combine them as you choose.
- To tweak how the Chrome Crayon effect appears, check the Modifier panel for node group controls, or use the in-scene gizmo interface for hands-on adjustments in the 3D view.

---

## Advanced Usage

- **Mixing with Other Assets**: Combine Chrome Crayon assets with Blender's other assets—use node groups to create advanced utilties never before seen by man.

- **Procedural Customization**: Add new parameters, dynamic generative properties, or animations to the geometry node group inputs or structure for unique, dynamic shapes and effects.

---

## Troubleshooting Common Geometry Node Group Issues

- **Asset Not Showing Up in Asset Browser**: 
  - Ensure your Chrome Crayon node group is saved and marked as an Asset (`Right-click → Mark as Asset`) in Blender.
  - Check that your Asset Library path is set up in Blender Preferences ([Blender Manual: Asset Libraries](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/editors/asset_browser.html#asset-libraries)).

- **Node Group Inputs Not Editable**:
  - If you can't see or adjust parameters, confirm the node group's inputs are properly exposed ([Blender: Exposing Group Inputs](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/modeling/geometry_nodes/geometry_node_group.html#inputs)).
  - In the Modifier panel, make sure any custom properties or controls are visible; unhide them in the node group if needed.

- **Unexpected Results or Errors**:
  - Use the recommended Blender version (5.0.0+). Geometry nodes may not be fully compatible across versions ([Blender: Geometry Nodes Versioning](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/modeling/geometry_nodes/index.html#versioning)).
  - Check for mismatched or missing node connections.

- **Slow or Laggy Viewport Performance**:
  - Node groups can increase computational load. Try reducing mesh resolution or disabling subdivisions.
  - Switch to the Eevee render engine for faster feedback ([Viewport Performance Tips](https://www.blender.org/manual/editors/3dview/navigate/viewports.html#viewport-performance)).
  - Hide heavy geometry or use “Simplify” viewport settings ([Blender Docs: Simplify](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/render/simplify.html)).

- **Inputs or Controls Missing in 3D View**:
  - If the custom gizmo interface isn’t visible, ensure the asset is selected and you’re in Object Mode or the correct workspace.
  - Custom gizmo features require Blender 3.6 or higher ([Blender: Custom Gizmos](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/advanced/gizmos/index.html)).

- **Broken Geometry or Mesh Artifacts**:
  - Clean up input curves to avoid overlapping or non-manifold segments ([Blender: Curve Editing](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/modeling/curves/editing/introduction.html)).
  - Use Blender’s “Clean Up” tools for curves and meshes ([Clean Up Mesh Tools](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/modeling/meshes/editing/cleanup.html)).

- **Asset Updates Not Showing in Browser**:
  - After making changes, re-save and re-mark the node group as an asset, then refresh the Asset Browser.
  - If problems persist, restart Blender or force a library reload ([Asset Browser Troubleshooting](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/editors/asset_browser.html#troubleshooting)).

> For additional support, see the [Blender Geometry Nodes Manual](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/modeling/geometry_nodes/) and [Asset Browser Documentation](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/editors/asset_browser.html), or search [Blender Stack Exchange](https://blender.stackexchange.com/questions/tagged/geometry-nodes) for community solutions.

---

## Tips and Best Practices

1. **Start Simple**: Begin by applying the asset with its default settings to see how it works out of the box. Once you’re comfortable, experiment by tweaking parameters and making more advanced adjustments for custom results.
2. **Customize and Save**: Tweak Chrome Crayon assets and save your own variations to your Asset Library. For guidance on making your own custom node groups, refer to [Blender's Creating Custom Node Groups documentation](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/modeling/geometry_nodes/geometry_node_group.html#creating-and-editing-node-groups).

---

## Support & Resources

- [Blender Asset Browser Documentation](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/editors/asset_browser.html)
- [Blender Geometry Nodes Manual](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/modeling/geometry_nodes/)
- [Node Dojo Manual](https://node-dojo-manual.readthedocs.io/)  <!-- Replace with actual URL if different -->

## Corner Mounted Skadis

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: OBJECT | Blender: 5.0+ | Tags: blender, 3d-printing, skadis, ikea, pegboard, organizer, corner-mount | Handle: corner-mounted-skadis

# Corner Mounted Skadis

## Description

A corner-mountable Skadis pegboard assembly for 3D printing. Mounts a Skadis panel at 90 degrees in a corner, allowing pegboard storage in tight spaces that flat-wall mounting cannot reach.

Static mesh asset — no configurable parameters. Drag and drop to use.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

## Dojo Bolt Gen v05

Type: Geometry Node | Asset: GEOMETRY | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: Node Group, hardware, 3D printable | Handle: dojo-bolt-gen-v05

Dojo Bolt Gen v05 is a flexible, procedural bolt generator for Blender designed to help makers and 3D artists create precise, customizable fasteners in minutes -- optimized for 3D printing as well as digital projects. With robust geometry nodes, this asset allows you to fine-tune every aspect -- thread profiles, head styles, shaft dimensions, chamfers, and more -- non-destructively, ensuring clean geometry suitable for physical fabrication.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOJO BOLT GEN V05.3                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                               Bolt Output    ○
│                                  BOLT GEO    ○
│                                       TAP    ○
│                               Thread ONLY    ○
│                          Only Head Sleeve    ○
│                            No Head Sleeve    ○
│                                Split Bolt    ○
│                                    T-Bolt    ○
│                                              │
□ Type                                         │
□ Print Ready                                  │
□ send to origin                               │
▮ length                   ⌖                   │
▮ thread dia               ⌖                   │
▮ head dia                 ⌖                   │
▮ head height              ⌖                   │
▮ head sides                                   │
▮ a untapped                                   │
□ thread only                                  │
□ TAP                                          │
▮ T-Bolt Cut Amount                            │
▮ tap tolerance                                │
├─ ▸ Filament/Cross Section Viewer ────────────┤
◉ Filament Color                               │
▮ layer height                                 │
□ cross section view                           │
▮ cut postion                                  │
■ Object                                       │
▮ x/y/z/custom                                 │
□ direction flip                               │
├─ ▸ extra ────────────────────────────────────┤
▮ bolt-head offset         ⌖                   │
▮ wall thic                ⌖                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Dojo Bolt Gen v05** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor or onto a mesh object
2. Set **Type** to choose the bolt configuration (Bolt 3-Part Assembly, etc.)
3. Adjust **length**, **thread dia**, **head dia**, and **head height** to define the bolt dimensions -- all gizmo-enabled for interactive editing
4. **head sides** controls the polygon count of the bolt head (6 = hex, higher = rounder)
5. **a untapped** sets the unthreaded portion of the shaft
6. Toggle **TAP** to generate a matching tap hole for the bolt
7. **tap tolerance** adds clearance between bolt and tap for printable fit
8. Toggle **Print Ready** to optimize the output for 3D printing
9. Multiple output sockets provide different bolt configurations: full bolt, thread only, head sleeve only, split bolt for printing, and T-bolt
10. Use the **Filament/Cross Section Viewer** panel to preview print layers and cross-section the bolt for inspection

## Changelog

### Version 05.3 — ~Dec 2025
- Multiple output sockets (Bolt Output, BOLT GEO, TAP, Thread ONLY, Head Sleeve, Split Bolt, T-Bolt)
- Filament/Cross Section Viewer panel for print layer preview
- Gizmo controls on length, thread dia, head dia, head height, bolt-head offset, wall thickness
- Print Ready toggle for optimized 3D print output
- send to origin toggle
- Extra panel: bolt-head offset + wall thickness controls
- Exported to no3d-tools storefront (2025-12-13), icons and demo media uploaded to Cloudinary
- Object version (Dojo Bolt Gen v05_Obj) packaged as separate drag-and-drop asset

### Version 05.2 — ~Nov 2025
- Exported from source file "NO3D TOOLS workbench_29NOV2025.blend"
- Prepared for no3d-tools library and website product listings

### v05 — Aug 2025
- Torx bolt head research (torx bolt.csv / .numbers — 2025-08-05)
- v5 thumb and key bolt variant printed (2025-08-11)

### v04 — Jun–Jul 2025
- Bolt blank variants and iterative print testing (bolt_blank_003–005, 2025-07-21)
- bolt7 variant printed (2025-07-21)
- bolt_v1 variant printed (2025-07-28)
- T-bolt feature added: half T-bolt and T-bolt turn adjusted variants (2025-06-23 – 2025-06-24)
- Extensive T-bolt print testing — 10+ test prints over Jul 7 (t_bolt_001–004)
- New bolt and half T-bolt first prints (2025-06-23)
- bolt_gen_v05_004 printed (2025-06-20) — first prints with v05 naming

### v03 — Mar 2025
- "new bolt generator features" development file (2025-03-01)
- Major update session: "Bolt Gen 3MAR2025 updates" (2025-03-03 – 2025-03-08)
- Continued iteration: "new bolt generator features 1MAR2026.blend" (mislabeled date, actually 2025-03-09 – 2025-03-15)
- Print testing: bolt_gen STLs exported (2025-03-03 – 2025-03-04)

### v02 — ~Apr 2025 (social post)
- Redesigned split-to-print cap orientation (cap flipped from top-down to bottom-up)
- Added T-bolt printing mode: flat-bottom symmetrical bolt, no assembly needed
- Apple Notes post (~Apr 14, 2025): addressed community feedback on V1 cap design
- Announced new cap direction for better mechanical advantage under fastening load

### v01 — May 2024
- "BOLT GEN _ splittable 3MAY2024" — first split-to-print feature (3-part assembly: 2 shaft halves + cap)
- First print tests (2024-05-06)
- "dojo hardware and bolt gen library 4MAY2024" — bundled with hardware library
- Bolt Object integration with shelf assembly bracket generator (2024-07-01 – 2024-07-02)
- "N03D TOOLS - 3D printing Utilities" bolt exports (2024-07-02)
- Split bolt demo video: "split bolt short.mov" (2024-07-25)

### V1 & V2 — Pre-2024
- Original "BOLT GENERATOR for Blender and Geometry Nodes V1 and V2" (.blend + .zip)
- Basic parametric bolt generator with geometry nodes
- Distributed as downloadable zip package

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

### Usage Guide

# To Use

## Getting into your scene:

This is specifically the Node Group version of the Bolt Generator that is primarily meant for quickly adding to the node editor.

To use just drag-and-drop this node group from your asset library into your node editor

<video controls src="https://res.cloudinary.com/dz6qxz9c4/video/upload/f_auto,q_auto/v1765693675/no3d-tools/dojo-bolt-gen-v05/bolt-gen-demo2.mov" title="Title"></video>
<video controls src="https://res.cloudinary.com/dz6qxz9c4/video/upload/f_auto,q_auto/v1766448721/no3d-tools/dojo-bolt-gen-v05/bolt-node-group-drop-demo.mov" title="Title"></video>

... Which can be especially handy for instancing the same bolt in several places in the node system:

<video controls src="https://res.cloudinary.com/dz6qxz9c4/video/upload/f_auto,q_auto/v1766448734/no3d-tools/dojo-bolt-gen-v05/instance-bolts-on-faces.mov" title="Title"></video>

## Modifying the Bolt

To modify bolt settings:
- Use Gizmos to directly modify bolt parameters in a more intuitive, free-flowing way
- use numerical parameter inputs in the group node to be more precise:
- plug parameters into node system for procedurally set bolt settings

<video controls src="https://res.cloudinary.com/dz6qxz9c4/video/upload/f_auto,q_auto/v1766448713/no3d-tools/dojo-bolt-gen-v05/bolt-group-gizmo-adjust.mov" title="Title"></video>
<video controls src="https://res.cloudinary.com/dz6qxz9c4/video/upload/f_auto,q_auto/v1766448729/no3d-tools/dojo-bolt-gen-v05/dojo-bolt-print-types.mov" title="Title"></video>

## 3D Printing Aides

There are multiple ways to print the bolt once it's ready.

<video controls src="https://res.cloudinary.com/dz6qxz9c4/video/upload/f_auto,q_auto/v1766448704/no3d-tools/dojo-bolt-gen-v05/animated-bolt-assembly.mov" title="Title"></video>
And these different bolt printing configurations are avaialble either from this dropdown or from the output sockets of the node group. That way mulitple different configs can be accessed in the node system without needing mulitple bolt node groups cluttering the editor:

<video controls src="https://res.cloudinary.com/dz6qxz9c4/video/upload/f_auto,q_auto/v1766448724/no3d-tools/dojo-bolt-gen-v05/bolt-printing-configs.mov" title="Title"></video>
- Split to Print
- T-bolts
-

---

## Dojo Bolt Gen v05_Obj

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: MESH | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset, PRocedural, Mesh Generator, 3D printable | Handle: dojo-bolt-gen-v05-obj

# Dojo Bolt Gen v05_Obj

## Description

The Object version of the now infamous Dojo Bolt Gen -- a drag-and-drop bolt asset that comes pre-configured as a scene object with all the same parametric controls as the node group version. This is the Bolt Gen v05.3 wrapped in a Geometry Nodes modifier on a ready-to-use object.

Note: The modifier on this object is a thin wrapper (Geometry in/out only). The full bolt parameters are exposed through the underlying **Dojo Bolt Gen v05.3** subgroup -- see the Dojo Bolt Gen v05 node group version for the complete parameter reference.

Now with gizmos enabled controls for easy modifications and customization!

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOJO BOLT GEN V05.3                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                               Bolt Output    ○
│                                  BOLT GEO    ○
│                                       TAP    ○
│                               Thread ONLY    ○
│                          Only Head Sleeve    ○
│                            No Head Sleeve    ○
│                                Split Bolt    ○
│                                    T-Bolt    ○
│                                              │
□ Type                                         │
□ Print Ready                                  │
□ send to origin                               │
▮ length                   ⌖                   │
▮ thread dia               ⌖                   │
▮ head dia                 ⌖                   │
▮ head height              ⌖                   │
▮ head sides                                   │
▮ a untapped                                   │
□ thread only                                  │
□ TAP                                          │
▮ T-Bolt Cut Amount                            │
▮ tap tolerance                                │
├─ ▸ Filament/Cross Section Viewer ────────────┤
◉ Filament Color                               │
▮ layer height                                 │
□ cross section view                           │
▮ cut postion                                  │
■ Object                                       │
▮ x/y/z/custom                                 │
□ direction flip                               │
├─ ▸ extra ────────────────────────────────────┤
▮ bolt-head offset         ⌖                   │
▮ wall thic                ⌖                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Dojo Bolt Gen v05_Obj** from the Asset Browser directly into your 3D scene
2. The bolt appears as a ready-to-use object with all parameters in the modifier panel
3. Adjust bolt dimensions using gizmos in the viewport or numerical inputs in the modifier panel
4. See the Dojo Bolt Gen v05 node group version documentation for full parameter details

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

### Usage Guide

# Dojo Bolt Gen v05_Obj

2. Open in Blender 5.0.0+
3. Import or drag the asset into your scene
4. Use the asset as needed

- Combine with other assets
- Use in your own projects

## Dojo Bool v5

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: GEOMETRY | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset | Handle: dojo-bool-v5

# Dojo Bool v5

## Description

A simple node group, but its in my top 5 most used node tools of all time.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOJO BOOL V5                         │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                           Geometry   ○
│                                      │
○ Mesh 1                               │
○ Mesh 2                               │
□ type                                 │
□ precision                            │
□ CUT                                  │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Dojo Bool v5** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. Connect **Mesh 1** and **Mesh 2** geometry inputs to the two meshes you want to boolean
3. Set **type** to choose the boolean operation: difference, intersect, or union
4. Set **precision** to select the solver mode: manifold (faster) or exact (more reliable)
5. Toggle **CUT** to slice both meshes along their intersection boundary instead of performing a solid boolean

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

## Dojo Calipers

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: MESH | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset | Handle: dojo-calipers

# Dojo Calipers

## Description

This is a very sensible and reasonable solution for getting accurate measurements of meshes within the Blender work environment.

This is a fully functional 3D simulation of a digital caliper that measures with +/- .001 mm accuracy.

If any machinist, engineer, or rocket scientist ever questions you using a free Animation or Video Game Asset development software for serious CAD modeling tasks, just send them a screen recodring of you using this baby to measure your mesh models. That'll shut them up.

Just don't forget the battery.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DIGITAL CALIPER CONTROLLER           │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                           Geometry   ○
│                                      │
○ Socket                               │
▮ Install Battery                      │
▮ measure               ⌖             │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag and drop the **Dojo Calipers** asset from the Asset Browser into your 3D scene
2. Insert the battery in the modifier panel by increasing **Install Battery** from 0 to 1
3. Note that the origin of this object is the very tip of the calipers jaws and is the 0 point of the measurement. Place that point at point A of the desired measurement span
4. Use the **measure** gizmo arrow to extend the upper jaw to point B of the desired measurement span
5. (Optional) Use your digital pen to write down on your digital note pad the captured measurement
6. (Mandatory) Proceed with your efficient and productive workday

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Dojo Crv Wrapper v4

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: GEOMETRY | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset | Handle: dojo-crv-wrapper-v4

# Dojo Crv Wrapper v4

## Description

A curve-wrapping utility that shrinkwraps a generated curve onto a target mesh surface, creating form-fitting outlines and contours.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOJO CRV WRAPPER V4                  │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                          geometry    ○
│                               pts    ○
│                              info    □
│                                      │
○ Subject Mesh                         │
▮ Tension factor                       │
▮ Wrap Factor                          │
▮ Resample Length                      │
▮ Wrap Iterations                      │
▮ Blur                                 │
▮ Offset              ⌖               │
□ Keep Geo                             │
□ corner pin                           │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Dojo Crv Wrapper v4** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. Connect **Subject Mesh** to the target mesh you want the curve to wrap around
3. **Tension factor** controls how tightly the curve conforms to the mesh surface
4. **Wrap Factor** sets the strength of each wrapping iteration (lower = softer drape)
5. **Resample Length** controls the curve point density (smaller = finer detail, slower)
6. **Wrap Iterations** sets how many passes the shrinkwrap performs (more = tighter fit)
7. **Blur** smooths the wrapped result to reduce jitter
8. **Offset** shifts the curve away from the surface (useful for clearance or layered wraps)
9. Toggle **Keep Geo** to include the original mesh in the output alongside the curve
10. Toggle **corner pin** to enable corner-pinning mode for tighter wrapping around sharp edges

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

Fixed grid box node group for snapping geometry to a grid with adjustable resolution and buffer

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOJO FIXED GRID                              │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                              │

▮ grid resolution                              │
▮ buffer                                       │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Dojo Gluefinity Grid

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: MESH | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset | Handle: dojo-gluefinity-grid

# Dojo Gluefinity Grid

## Description

A flexible glue grid generator for 3D printing. Creates interlocking grid patterns that can be printed flat and used as flexible connectors or organizational trays. Supports stacking configurations and customizable grid sizes.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ FLEXIGLUEGRID                        │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                           Geometry   ○
│                                      │
○ Geometry                             │
□ STACK                                │
▮ Size X                   ⌖           │
▮ Size Y                   ⌖           │
◉ color                                │
□ Randomize Grid colors                │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Dojo Gluefinity Grid** from the Asset Browser into your scene
2. **Size X** and **Size Y** control the overall grid dimensions
3. Toggle **STACK** to enable a stacking configuration for multi-layer grids
4. Set **color** to change the grid's display color for visualization
5. Toggle **Randomize Grid colors** to assign random colors per grid cell for easier identification

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

### Usage Guide

# Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj - Asset User Guide

## Getting Started

### Installation

1. **Download the Asset**
   - Locate the Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj `.blend` file in your downloads or asset library.
   - Ensure you have Blender 5.0.0 or later installed.

### Adding Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj to a New Asset Library

To create a dedicated library for NO3D assets:

1. **Create a New Asset Library Folder**
   - In your file system, create a new folder and name it **no3d tools library** (or a name you'll recognize).
2. **Add Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj**
   - Copy `Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj.blend` into your new **no3d tools library** folder.

3. **Register the Library in Blender**
   - In Blender, go to **Edit > Preferences > File Paths > Asset Libraries**.
   - Click **Add**, then select your new **no3d tools library** folder. Click **Accept** and close Preferences.

Now, in Blender's Asset Browser, choose **no3d tools library** from the Asset Libraries dropdown. Your Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj assets are ready to use—just drag them into your project!

---

### Adding Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj to an Existing Asset Library

If you already have an asset library folder (for example, **no3d tools library**):

1. Copy `Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj.blend` into your existing **no3d tools library** folder.
2. Open `Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj.blend` in Blender.
3. Mark your desired assets (**Mark as Asset**), and save the file.
4. In Blender's Asset Browser, make sure your asset library is selected. Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj assets will now appear—drag them into your project whenever you need them!

That's it!

---

## Using the Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj Asset

### Drag-and-Drop from Asset Browser

1. **Open Asset Browser**
   - Go to the Asset Browser panel in Blender (accessible in the main window, or via Area change).

2. **Find Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj**
   - Look in the section or category (such as Materials, Node Groups, or Objects) where you saved the Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj asset.

3. **Use the Asset**
   - For materials: drag and drop Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj onto your selected object in the 3D viewport.
   - For node groups: drag directly into the Geometry Nodes Editor.
   - For objects: drag into your scene collection.

---

## Customizing the Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj Asset

Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj assets are fully customizable and designed to help you create Gridfinity-compatible grids that perfectly fit your workspace.

### Creating Custom Grid Sizes

1. **Measure Your Space**
   - Before creating your grid, measure the dimensions of your desk, shelf, or drawer where you want to place the Gridfinity grid.

2. **Adjust Grid Parameters**
   - To customize parameters, look in the **Modifier panel** when the node group is added as a modifier—there you'll find all adjustable settings for grid dimensions, spacing, and cell count.
   - OR use the included **custom gizmo interface** that appears in the 3D Viewport when the asset is selected, allowing interactive adjustment of key parameters.

3. **Preview and Refine**
   - The geometry updates in real-time as you adjust parameters, so you can see exactly how your custom grid will look before printing.

### For Objects

- Imported Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj objects come fully set up with the geometry applied. You can duplicate, modify, or combine them as you choose.
- To tweak how the Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj appears, check the Modifier panel for node group controls, or use the in-scene gizmo interface for hands-on adjustments in the 3D view.

---

## Advanced Usage

### Multiple Grid Generation

- **Mixing with Other Assets**: Combine Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj assets with Blender's other assets—use node groups to create advanced organizational systems.
- **Procedural Customization**: Add new parameters, dynamic generative properties, or animations to the geometry node group inputs or structure for unique, dynamic grid layouts.
- **Workspace Planning**: Generate multiple grid sizes in a single scene to plan complete workspace organization systems before printing.

### Gridfinity Integration

- This product generates grids compatible with the Gridfinity modular storage system.
- Use the generated grids as bases for organizing tools, components, and accessories using standard Gridfinity bins and accessories.
- Combine with other Gridfinity-compatible designs for comprehensive storage solutions.

---

## Troubleshooting Common Geometry Node Group Issues

- **Asset Not Showing Up in Asset Browser**: 
  - Ensure your Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj node group is saved and marked as an Asset (`Right-click → Mark as Asset`) in Blender.
  - Check that your Asset Library path is set up in Blender Preferences ([Blender Manual: Asset Libraries](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/editors/asset_browser.html#asset-libraries)).

- **Node Group Inputs Not Editable**:
  - If you can't see or adjust parameters, confirm the node group's inputs are properly exposed ([Blender: Exposing Group Inputs](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/modeling/geometry_nodes/geometry_node_group.html#inputs)).
  - In the Modifier panel, make sure any custom properties or controls are visible; unhide them in the node group if needed.

- **Unexpected Results or Errors**:
  - Use the recommended Blender version (5.0.0+). Geometry nodes may not be fully compatible across versions ([Blender: Geometry Nodes Versioning](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/modeling/geometry_nodes/index.html#versioning)).
  - Check for mismatched or missing node connections.

- **Slow or Laggy Viewport Performance**:
  - Node groups can increase computational load. Try reducing mesh resolution or disabling subdivisions.
  - Switch to the Eevee render engine for faster feedback ([Viewport Performance Tips](https://www.blender.org/manual/editors/3dview/navigate/viewports.html#viewport-performance)).
  - Hide heavy geometry or use "Simplify" viewport settings ([Blender Docs: Simplify](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/render/simplify.html)).

- **Inputs or Controls Missing in 3D View**:
  - If the custom gizmo interface isn't visible, ensure the asset is selected and you're in Object Mode or the correct workspace.
  - Custom gizmo features require Blender 3.6 or higher ([Blender: Custom Gizmos](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/advanced/gizmos/index.html)).

- **Broken Geometry or Mesh Artifacts**:
  - Clean up input geometry to avoid overlapping or non-manifold segments.
  - Use Blender's "Clean Up" tools for meshes ([Clean Up Mesh Tools](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/modeling/meshes/editing/cleanup.html)).

- **Asset Updates Not Showing in Browser**:
  - After making changes, re-save and re-mark the node group as an asset, then refresh the Asset Browser.
  - If problems persist, restart Blender or force a library reload ([Asset Browser Troubleshooting](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/editors/asset_browser.html#troubleshooting)).

> For additional support, see the [Blender Geometry Nodes Manual](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/modeling/geometry_nodes/) and [Asset Browser Documentation](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/editors/asset_browser.html), or search [Blender Stack Exchange](https://blender.stackexchange.com/questions/tagged/geometry-nodes) for community solutions.

---

## Tips and Best Practices

1. **Start Simple**: Begin by applying the asset with its default settings to see how it works out of the box. Once you're comfortable, experiment by tweaking parameters to create grids that match your specific workspace dimensions.

2. **Measure First**: Before generating your grid, accurately measure your desk, shelf, or drawer to ensure the grid will fit perfectly. Account for any tolerances needed for 3D printing.

3. **Customize and Save**: Tweak Dojo Gluefinity Grid_obj assets and save your own variations to your Asset Library. For guidance on making your own custom node groups, refer to [Blender's Creating Custom Node Groups documentation](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/modeling/geometry_nodes/geometry_node_group.html#creating-and-editing-node-groups).

4. **Test Print Settings**: Before printing large grids, test with a small section to verify your printer settings and ensure proper adhesion and dimensional accuracy.

---

## About Gridfinity

The Gridfinity system was originally created by **Zack Freedman** (Voidstar Lab) as a free, open-source modular workshop organization solution. This product builds upon that foundation to provide enhanced customization capabilities within Blender.

**Credit and Resources:**
- **Original Creator**: [Zack Freedman](https://www.youtube.com/@VoidstarLab) (Voidstar Lab)
- **Original Gridfinity Video**: [Gridfinity: Your Ultimate Modular Workshop is FREE!](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ra_9zU-mnl8)
- **Gridfinity Resources**: Visit [Zack Freedman's YouTube Channel](https://www.youtube.com/@VoidstarLab) for tutorials, updates, and the latest Gridfinity designs

---

## Support & Resources

- [Blender Asset Browser Documentation](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/editors/asset_browser.html)
- [Blender Geometry Nodes Manual](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/modeling/geometry_nodes/)
- [Node Dojo Manual](https://node-dojo-manual.readthedocs.io/)
- [Gridfinity Original System](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ra_9zU-mnl8) - Learn about the original Gridfinity system by Zack Freedman

---

## Dojo Gridfinity Bins

Type: Blender Object | Asset: OBJECT | Blender: 4.0.0+ | Tags: blender, geometry-nodes, 3D-printable, organizer, gridfinity | Handle: dojo-gridfinity-bins

Parametric Gridfinity-compatible bin generator with adjustable X/Y size and height

## Parameters

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOJO GRIDFINITY BINS                         │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                              │
■ Geometry                          Geometry ○ │
▮ Size X                      ⌖               │
▮ Size Y                      ⌖               │
▮ bin z                       ⌖               │
◉ color                                        │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)

## Dojo Knob

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: OBJECT | Blender: 5.0+ | Tags: blender, geometry-nodes, super-primitive | Handle: dojo-knob

# Dojo Knob

## Description

A parametric knob generator node group. Creates an odd-looking but incredibly useful shape for all kinds of highly functional mechanical parts and 3D printable objects -- control interfaces, furniture hardware, mechanical assemblies, and more.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOJO KNOB                            │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                           Geometry   ○
│                                      │
○ Geometry                             │
▮ Resolution                           │
▮ head width               ⌖           │
▮ head height              ⌖           │
▮ sink depth               ⌖           │
▮ shaft width              ⌖           │
▮ shaft length             ⌖           │
▮ pill length              ⌖           │
□ center                               │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Dojo Knob** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. Connect **Geometry** to an input mesh (or leave empty for standalone generation)
3. **head width** and **head height** define the knob cap dimensions
4. **shaft width** and **shaft length** set the mounting post size
5. **sink depth** controls how deep the shaft recesses into the head
6. **pill length** rounds the shaft into a pill/capsule shape (0 = cylinder)
7. **Resolution** controls mesh density (higher = smoother curves)
8. Toggle **center** to align the knob origin to the center of the head or the base of the shaft

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Dojo Knob_obj

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: MESH | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: Mesh Gen, blender, 3d, asset | Handle: dojo-knob-obj

# Dojo Knob_obj

## Description

A parametric generator for an odd-looking but increadibly useful shape for creating all kinds of highly functional mechanical parts and useful 3D pritnable objects. See also: printable control interfaces, furniture hardware, or mechanical assemblies.

Now updated with 6 easily to use gizmo controls!
## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOJO KNOB                            │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                           Geometry   ○
│                                      │
○ Geometry                             │
▮ Resolution                           │
▮ Head Width     ⌖                     │
▮ Head Height    ⌖                     │
▮ Sink Depth     ⌖                     │
▮ Shaft Width    ⌖                     │
▮ Shaft Length   ⌖                     │
▮ Pill Length    ⌖                     │
□ Center                               │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Dojo Knob** from the Asset Browser into your scene
2. The modifier panel exposes all parameters listed above
3. Set **Head Width** and **Head Height** to define the knob cap dimensions
4. Set **Shaft Width** and **Shaft Length** for the mounting post
5. **Sink Depth** controls how deep the shaft recesses into the head
6. **Pill Length** rounds the shaft into a pill/capsule shape (0 = cylinder)
7. **Resolution** controls mesh density (higher = smoother curves)
8. Toggle **Center** to align the knob origin to the center of the head or the base of the shaft

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Dojo Mesh Repair

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: GEOMETRY | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset | Handle: dojo-mesh-repair

# Dojo Mesh Repair

## Description

A diagnostic node group that checks mesh geometry for non-manifold edges and other print-readiness issues. Highlights problem areas with visual markers so you can fix them before exporting.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOJO MESH REPAIR                     │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                            Output    ○
│                            errors    ▮
│                             Curve    ○
│                              Show    □
│                                      │
○ Input                                │
□ show non-manifold edges              │
□ show nm edges only                   │
▮ marker size              ⌖           │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Dojo Mesh Repair** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. Connect **Input** to the mesh you want to inspect
3. Toggle **show non-manifold edges** to highlight edges that are not watertight
4. Toggle **show nm edges only** to hide the mesh and show only the problem edges
5. Adjust **marker size** to scale the visual error indicators for visibility
6. The **errors** output provides a count of non-manifold edges found
7. The **Curve** output provides the non-manifold edges as curve geometry for further processing

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Dojo Plywood Generator

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: GEOMETRY | Blender: 4.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset, material, geometry-nodes, plywood, procedural | Handle: dojo-plywood-generator

# Dojo Plywood Generator

## Description

Procedural plywood cross-section effect applied through geometry nodes. Adds realistic layered plywood edge appearance to any mesh. Control grain rotation, texture scale, layer visibility, and whether side faces get the treatment too.

Originally built as "dojo_plyoood gn" for the Dojo Skaddis pegboard project — now extracted as a standalone utility for any box, shelf, or panel that needs that satisfying plywood edge look.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOJO PLYWOOD GENERATOR               │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                           Geometry   ○
│                                      │
○ Geometry                             │
▮ sides                    = on        │
□ rot                      = 0.5       │
□ scale                    = 0.075     │
□ layers                   = 0.725     │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Dojo Plywood Generator** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. Connect your mesh to the **Geometry** input
3. Toggle **sides** to include or exclude side faces from the plywood effect
4. Adjust **rot** to rotate the grain direction of the plywood layers
5. Set **scale** to control the overall texture scale of the wood grain
6. Dial **layers** to control the visibility/density of the alternating plywood layers

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial library release (extracted from Dojo Skaddis 27jan2026.blend)
- 19-node geometry node group
- Fixed product name typo from "dojo_plyoood gn"

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Dojo Print Viz_V4.5

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: GEOMETRY | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset | Handle: dojo-print-viz-v45

# Dojo Print Viz_V4.5

## Description

A 3D print visualization tool that simulates filament layer lines and cross-section views on any mesh. Useful for previewing how a model will look when printed before exporting to a slicer.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOJO PRINT VIZ V4.5                      │
├──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                               Geometry   ○
│                                 Output   ○
│                                          │
○ Input                                    │
◉ color                                    │
□ cross section view                       │
□ direction flip                           │
▮ xyz - cross section                      │
▮ cut postion                              │
▮ roughness                                │
▮ strength                                 │
▮ distance                                 │
■ Object                                   │
▮ Layer Height                             │
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Dojo Print Viz_V4.5** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. Connect **Input** to the mesh you want to preview
3. Set **color** to match your filament color for realistic visualization
4. **Layer Height** controls the simulated print layer thickness (e.g., 0.2mm)
5. Toggle **cross section view** to slice the model and see the interior
6. **xyz - cross section** selects which axis to cut along (0=X, 1=Y, 2=Z)
7. **cut postion** slides the cross-section plane along the selected axis
8. **direction flip** reverses the cross-section cut direction
9. **roughness** and **strength** control the filament material appearance
10. **distance** adjusts the layer line visual effect intensity
11. Optionally assign an **Object** for custom cross-section plane orientation

## Changelog

### Version 4.5
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Dojo Squircle v4.5_obj

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: MESH | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: Mesh Gen, blender, addon, 3d, asset | Handle: dojo-squircle-v45-obj

# Dojo Squircle v4.5_obj

## Description

The Object version of the Dojo Squircle -- a drag-and-drop rounded rectangle generator that creates box shapes with smooth corner radii and wall thickness. Ready to use as a scene object with all parameters in the modifier panel.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOJO SQUIRCLE V4.5                   │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                           Geometry   ○
│                                      │
▮ Size X                   ⌖           │
▮ Size Y                   ⌖           │
▮ Size Z (wall height)     ⌖           │
▮ Wall Thickness           ⌖           │
▮ Corner Radius            ⌖           │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Dojo Squircle v4.5_obj** from the Asset Browser directly into your 3D scene
2. **Size X** and **Size Y** set the footprint dimensions of the squircle
3. **Size Z (wall height)** controls the extrusion height
4. **Wall Thickness** hollows the shape to create a container (0 = solid)
5. **Corner Radius** controls how rounded the corners are (0 = sharp square, higher = rounder)
6. All parameters are gizmo-enabled for interactive 3D viewport editing

## Changelog

### Version 4.5
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Dojo_Curvature Lines

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: GEOMETRY | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset | Handle: dojo-curvature-lines

# Dojo Curvature Lines

## Description

Generates curvature-following line instances along a curve, creating hatching or contour-line effects that follow the natural flow of the input curve geometry. Use to easily analyze curvature visually, or to create spikey effects! Whatever!

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOJO_CURVATURE LINES                 │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                         Instances    ○
│                                      │
○ Curve                                │
□ reverse lines                        │
▮ curve line length        ⌖           │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **dojo_curvature lines** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. Connect **Curve** to the input curve you want to generate curvature lines from
3. **curve line length** controls the length of each generated line segment
4. Toggle **reverse lines** to flip the direction of the generated line instances

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Dojo_Fillet by Length

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: GEOMETRY | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset | Handle: dojo-fillet-by-length

# Dojo Fillet by Length

## Description

A curve fillet tool that rounds sharp corners on curves using a length-based radius, giving more predictable results than Blender's built-in fillet which uses a factor-based approach.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOJO_FILLET BY LENGTH                │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                             Curve    ○
│                                      │
○ Curve                                │
▮ radius                   ⌖           │
▮ resample length                      │
├─ ▸ Pt Select ───────────────────────┤
□ Only Selected Points                 │
▮ Corner Pt Select                     │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **dojo_fillet by length** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. Connect **Curve** to the input curve with sharp corners you want to fillet
3. **radius** sets the fillet radius in scene units (e.g., millimeters)
4. **resample length** controls the point density of the filleted curve (smaller = smoother arcs)
5. Use the **Pt Select** panel to fillet only specific corner points:
   - Toggle **Only Selected Points** to limit the fillet to selected corners
   - **Corner Pt Select** picks a specific point index to fillet

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Dojo_Squircle v4.5

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: GEOMETRY | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: Super Primitive, blender, addon, 3d, asset | Handle: dojo-squircle-v45

# Dojo Squircle v4.5

## Description

A parametric squircle (rounded rectangle) generator node group. Creates box shapes with smooth, controllable corner radii and wall thickness -- perfect for enclosures, cases, and containers.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOJO_SQUIRCLE V4.5                   │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                           Geometry   ○
│                                      │
▮ Size X                   ⌖           │
▮ Size Y                   ⌖           │
▮ Size Z (wall height)     ⌖           │
▮ Wall Thickness           ⌖           │
▮ Corner Radius            ⌖           │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Dojo_Squircle v4.5** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. **Size X** and **Size Y** set the footprint dimensions of the squircle
3. **Size Z (wall height)** controls the extrusion height
4. **Wall Thickness** hollows the shape to create a container (0 = solid)
5. **Corner Radius** controls how rounded the corners are (0 = sharp square, higher = rounder)

## Changelog

### Version 4.5
- Initial release
- Core functionality

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## Dowel Shelf Generator

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: OBJECT | Blender: 4.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset, generator, geometry-nodes, shelf, furniture, 3d-printable | Handle: dowel-shelf-generator

# Dowel Shelf Generator

## Description

A parametric shelf generator that creates dowel-joined shelving units entirely through geometry nodes. Set the overall width, depth, and height, choose how many shelves, and adjust the material thickness and dowel diameter. Toggle dimension annotations on or off.

Drop it in, dial in your dimensions, and export for 3D printing or use as a reference for a real woodworking project. The dowel joints are modeled as solid geometry — ready for boolean operations or direct STL export.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DOWEL SHELF GENERATOR                │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                           Geometry   ○
│                                      │
○ Geometry                             │
□ Width                    = 2.0    ⌖ │
□ Depth                    = 2.0    ⌖ │
□ Height                   = 0.5    ⌖ │
□ shelf thiccness          = 10.38     │
□ Dowel Diameter           = 0.5       │
■ no of shelves            = 4         │
▮ dims                     = off       │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag the **Dowel Shelf Generator** object from the Asset Browser into your 3D scene
2. Set **Width**, **Depth**, and **Height** to define the overall shelf dimensions (gizmo-enabled for viewport dragging)
3. Adjust **shelf thiccness** for the thickness of each shelf panel
4. Set **Dowel Diameter** for the connecting dowel rod thickness
5. Change **no of shelves** to add or remove shelf levels
6. Toggle **dims** to show or hide dimension annotations in the viewport

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial library release (extracted from putty modeling experiment.blend)
- 67-node geometry node group
- Object asset with modifier-based parametric control

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Flat Color Material

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: GEOMETRY_NODE_MATERIAL | Blender: 5.0+ | Tags: blender, material, shader, flat-color, emission, viewport, geometry-nodes | Handle: flat-color-material

# Flat Color Material

## Description

A Geometry Nodes modifier that applies a flat emission color to any mesh. Drag it onto an object for instant flat-shaded viewport preview — no material setup needed. Useful for render overlays, non-photorealistic shading, color-coded visualizations, and quick prototyping.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ FLAT COLOR MATERIAL                  │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                           Geometry   ○
│                                      │
○ Geometry                             │
▮ Emit Factor                          │
◉ Color                                │
□ Selection                            │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Flat Color Material** from the Asset Browser onto any mesh object
2. The modifier applies a flat emission shader to the geometry
3. Set **Color** to choose the flat color (default: Lello #f0ff00)
4. Adjust **Emit Factor** to control emission brightness (1.0 = full, 0.0 = black)
5. Use **Selection** to apply the material to specific faces only (True = all faces)

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Flat emission color via Geometry Nodes modifier

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

▮ Gabor Scale                                  │
▮ Blur                                         │
□ Show Case                                    │

## Metabones Obj

Type: Mesh Object | Asset: MESH | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset, procedural, mesh generator, 3D printable, metabones | Handle: metabones-obj

# Metabones Obj

## Description

A 2D metaball-style bone connector generator. Creates smooth, organic shapes by blending circles with arc tangent connections -- useful for creating bone-like structures, organic connectors, and flowing 2D shapes.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 2D METABONES                         │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                           Geometry   ○
│                                      │
○ Geometry                             │
▮ Radius                   ⌖           │
▮ stretch                              │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Metabones Obj** from the Asset Browser into your scene
2. **Radius** controls the size of the metaball circles
3. **stretch** adjusts the blending factor between connected circles (0 = tight, 1 = stretched)

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## No3d Knurler

Type: Geometry Node | Asset: GEOMETRY | Blender: 4.0.0+ | Tags: blender, geometry-nodes, surface-detail, knurling | Handle: no3d-knurler

Procedural knurling generator for adding grip texture patterns to cylindrical surfaces

│ NO3D KNURLER                                 │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                              │

## No3d Pixel Markers

Type: Mesh Object | Asset: MESH | Blender: 4.2.0+ | Tags: UI, Marker, Utility, Mograph, Free | Handle: no3d-pixel-markers

# No3d Pixel Markers

## Description

A pixel art-style marker generator that converts curves into pixelated, blocky geometry. Can produce both 3D extruded pixel forms and flat 2D pixel art, with customizable resolution and color controls.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ PIXEL MARKER                             │
├──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                               Geometry   ○
│                                          │
○ Geometry                                 │
▮ extrude height           ⌖              │
▮ line thiccness           ⌖              │
□ FLATTEN                                  │
▮ pixel resolution                         │
□ 3D/flat                                  │
◉ Flat Color                               │
◉ 3d color                                 │
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **No3d Pixel Markers** from the Asset Browser onto a curve object in your scene
2. **extrude height** controls how tall the pixel blocks are extruded in 3D mode
3. **line thiccness** sets the width of the pixel stroke
4. Toggle **FLATTEN** to collapse the pixels to a flat plane
5. **pixel resolution** controls the pixel grid density (smaller = more pixels, finer detail)
6. Toggle **3D/flat** to switch between 3D extruded pixels and flat pixel art
7. **Flat Color** sets the emission color in flattened mode
8. **3d color** sets the material color in 3D mode

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

Parametric nylon bolt generator with adjustable length, cap option, and multiple output configurations

│                                              │

│                             bool block    ○  │
│                         bool cylinder    ○   │
│                            Z Dimension    ○  │
□ Menu                                         │
▮ Length                                       │

□ dims                                         │

```

## Print Bed Preview_obj

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: MESH | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset | Handle: print-bed-preview-obj

# Print Bed Preview

## Description

A 3D print bed simulator that shows your model sitting on a real-world-scale print bed. Includes bed size presets, slice preview, and object transform controls for positioning your model before export.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ PRINT BED PREVIEW                        │
├──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                               Geometry   ○
│                                          │
○ Geometry                                 │
□ Menu (bed size)                          │
▮ Size Select                              │
■ Select Object to Print                   │
◉ Filament Color                           │
├─ ▸ SLICE PREVIEW ───────────────────────┤
▮ LAYER                                    │
□ slice                                    │
├─ ▸ OBJECT TRANSFORM ───────────────────┤
▮ Scale                                    │
◇ Translation                              │
◇ Rotation                                 │
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Print Bed Preview** from the Asset Browser into your scene
2. Select a **bed size** preset from the Menu dropdown (e.g., 200mm, 300mm)
3. Assign your model to **Select Object to Print** to see it positioned on the bed
4. Set **Filament Color** to preview the printed color
5. Use the **SLICE PREVIEW** panel to simulate layer slicing:
   - **LAYER** scrubs through print layers
   - Toggle **slice** to enable/disable the slice view
6. Use the **OBJECT TRANSFORM** panel to adjust **Scale**, **Translation**, and **Rotation** of the print object on the bed

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Print Orientation

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: OBJECT | Blender: 5.0+ | Tags: blender, geometry-nodes, 3d-printing, utility | Handle: print-orientation

# Print Orientation

## Description

A utility that orients a mesh for 3D printing by selecting which face should sit flat on the print bed. Simply select a face and the mesh rotates to place that face down -- no more guessing at rotation angles for optimal print orientation.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ORIENT PRINT BY FACE                 │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                     oriented mesh    ○
│                                      │
○ Input                                │
□ Surface Selection                    │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Print Orientation** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. Connect **Input** to the mesh you want to orient for printing
3. Set **Surface Selection** to select the face that should be the print bed contact surface (use a face selection attribute or boolean)
4. The **oriented mesh** output provides the rotated geometry with the selected face flat on the XY plane

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Putty Flange Generator

Type: Blender Object | Asset: OBJECT | Blender: 4.0.0+ | Tags: blender, geometry-nodes, putty, procedural | Handle: putty-flange-generator

Procedural putty flange generator that molds organic flanged shapes around target objects

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ PUTTY FLANGE GENERATOR                       │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                              │
■ Geometry                          Geometry ○ │
□ target objects                               │
▮ Putty mass                                   │
▮ Soften                                       │
◉ Filament Color                               │
▮ Putty Factor                                 │
▮ mold factor                                  │
▮ thiccness                                    │
□ flange shape                                 │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```
