## Screwdriver

Type: Blender Object | Asset: OBJECT | Blender: 4.0.0+ | Tags: blender, geometry-nodes, hardware, reference-model | Handle: screwdriver

Screwdriver model with geometry nodes assembly

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ SCREWDRIVER                                  │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                              │
■ Geometry                          Geometry ○ │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Select By Bounding Face

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: GEOMETRY | Blender: 4.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset, selection, geometry-nodes | Handle: select-by-bounding-face

# Select By Bounding Face

## Description

Select faces on a mesh by their proximity to a bounding box face. Feed in a geometry, pick which bounding face to target (by index), and dial in a distance threshold. The node outputs the geometry with the selection applied and a boolean result for downstream logic.

Great for quickly isolating the top, bottom, or side faces of any mesh based on its bounding box — no manual selection needed.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ SELECT BY BOUNDING FACE.V2           │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                               face   ○
│                             Result   ○
│                                      │
○ Geometry                             │
□ face select              = 2         │
□ dist                     = 0.01      │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Select By Bounding Face** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. Connect your mesh to the **Geometry** input
3. Set **face select** to choose which bounding box face to target (0-5 maps to the six faces of the bounding box)
4. Adjust **dist** to control the distance threshold — faces within this distance of the chosen bounding face will be selected
5. Use the **face** output for the geometry with the selection applied, or the **Result** boolean for further logic

## Changelog

### Version 2.0.0
- Initial library release (extracted from WIP nodes)
- 11-node geometry node group

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Select Half Of Object

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: GEOMETRY | Blender: 4.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset, selection, geometry-nodes | Handle: select-half-of-object

# Select Half Of Object

## Description

Select exactly half of a mesh along any axis. Choose X, Y, or Z from the menu, flip the direction with the +/- toggle, and slide the Factor to adjust the split position. Outputs the geometry with the selection applied and a boolean result.

Perfect for symmetry workflows — select one half, delete it, mirror the other, or just isolate geometry for targeted operations.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ SELECT HALF OF OBJECT                │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                               face   ○
│                             Result   ○
│                                      │
○ Geometry                             │
◇ Menu                     = x        │
▮ +/-                      = off       │
□ Factor                   = 0.5       │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Select Half Of Object** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. Connect your mesh to the **Geometry** input
3. Choose the split axis from the **Menu** dropdown (x, y, or z)
4. Toggle **+/-** to flip which half is selected (positive or negative direction)
5. Adjust **Factor** to move the split point — 0.5 is the exact midpoint, lower values select less, higher values select more
6. Use the **face** geometry output or the **Result** boolean for downstream operations

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial library release (extracted from WIP nodes)
- 36-node geometry node group

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Separate Half

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: GEOMETRY | Blender: 4.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset, selection, geometry-nodes, split | Handle: separate-half

# Separate Half

## Description

Split a mesh in half and get both halves as separate geometry outputs. Pick an axis, choose the direction, adjust the split factor, and select the domain (point, edge, face, etc). The Selection output gets the chosen half, the face output gets the other half, and the Result boolean can drive further logic.

This is the destructive sibling of Select Half Of Object — instead of just selecting, it actually separates the geometry into two streams.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ SEPARATE HALF                        │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                          Selection   ○
│                               face   ○
│                             Result   ○
│                                      │
○ Input                                │
◇ Menu                     = z        │
▮ +/-                      = off       │
□ Factor                   = 0.5       │
◇ Menu                     = point     │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Separate Half** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. Connect your mesh to the **Input**
3. Choose the split axis from the first **Menu** (x, y, or z)
4. Toggle **+/-** to flip which half goes to which output
5. Adjust **Factor** to move the split point (0.5 = midpoint)
6. Choose the separation domain from the second **Menu** (point, edge, face, etc.)
7. Wire the **Selection** and **face** outputs to separate geometry streams

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial library release (extracted from WIP nodes)
- 13-node geometry node group

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Set Holdout Material

Type: Material | Asset: MATERIAL | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: blender, material, 3d, asset, holdout, compositing | Handle: set-holdout-material

# Set Holdout Material

## Description

A simple utility node group that applies a holdout material to selected geometry within geometry nodes. Holdout materials make objects invisible in renders while still blocking light and reflections -- useful for compositing and masking workflows.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ SET HOLDOUT MATERIAL                 │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                           Geometry   ○
│                                      │
○ Geometry                             │
□ Selection                            │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Set Holdout Material** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. Connect **Geometry** to the mesh you want to make holdout
3. Use **Selection** to limit the holdout effect to specific faces (True = all faces)

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

Parametric bin/organizer generator with adjustable dimensions, dividers, and corner fillets

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ SIMPLE BIN GENERATOR                         │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                              │
■ Geometry                          Geometry ○ │
▮ width                       ⌖               │
▮ depth                       ⌖               │
▮ Height of bins              ⌖               │
▮ divide iterations                            │
▮ wall thiccness              ⌖               │
▮ corner fillet                                │
▮ margin                                       │
▮ X                                            │
▮ Y                                            │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

```
Scene file with a complete procedural notebook/sketchbook system.
```

## Spike Ball_Simple V1

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: MESH | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset, procedural, mesh generator, 3D printable | Handle: spike-ball-simple-v1

# Spike Ball Simple V1

## Description

A parametric spike ball generator that creates a sphere covered in evenly-distributed spikes. Outputs the combined spike ball as well as separate spike and ball geometry for flexible compositing.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ SPIKE BALL_SIMPLE V1                 │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                        spike ball    ○
│                            spikes    ○
│                              ball    ○
│                                      │
▮ ball radius              ⌖           │
▮ spike radius             ⌖           │
▮ spike length             ⌖           │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Spike Ball_Simple V1** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. **ball radius** sets the size of the central sphere
3. **spike radius** controls the thickness of each spike at its base
4. **spike length** sets how far each spike extends from the ball surface
5. Use the separate outputs (**spike ball**, **spikes**, **ball**) to apply different materials or process each part independently

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

## Spike Putty 1

Type: Blender Object | Asset: OBJECT | Blender: 4.0.0+ | Tags: blender, geometry-nodes, putty, procedural | Handle: spike-putty-1

Procedural spike putty wrapper that creates organic spiked surfaces around mesh collections

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ SPIKE PUTTY 1                                │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                              │
│                               Geometry    ○  │
│                                  Value    ○  │
□ Collection                                   │
▮ Voxel size                                   │
▮ Hole count                                   │
▮ Blur/Relax surface iterations                │
▮ Strand Radius              ⌖                 │
▮ hi res                                       │
▮ Tigthen to Surface                           │
▮ Spike Length                                  │
▮ spike base width            ⌖                │
▮ dull/sharp                                   │
◉ color                                        │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## T-Slot Utilities

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: OBJECT | Blender: 5.0+ | Tags: blender, geometry-nodes, utility, mechanical | Handle: t-slot-utilities

# T-Slot Utilities

## Description

A T-slot aluminum extrusion generator with a library of standard profiles (20x20, 20x40, etc.). Extrudes profiles along a mesh line with cost estimation, remeshing, and polygon size controls. Includes both the extrusion generator and a standalone profile library node group.

## Parameters

### T-slot Extrusion (mesh line)

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ T-SLOT EXTRUSION_MESH LINE               │
├──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                               Geometry   ○
│                               ext size   ▮
│                                   cost   ▮
│                                          │
○ Geometry                                 │
▮ poly size                                │
□ remesh                                   │
□ Menu (profile size)                      │
▮ cost per mm                              │
□ custom cost                              │
□ disp cost                                │
◇ Offset                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### T-slot Profiles (standalone)

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ T-SLOT PROFILES                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                               Geometry   ○
│                               ext size   ▮
│                                   cost   ▮
│                                          │
□ Menu (profile size)                      │
□ custom cost                              │
▮ cost per mm                              │
□ Name                                     │
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Usage

1. Drag **T-slot Extrusion_mesh line** from the Asset Browser onto a mesh line in your scene
2. Select the extrusion **profile size** from the Menu dropdown (20x20, 20x40, etc.)
3. **poly size** controls the mesh polygon density of the extruded profile
4. Toggle **remesh** to apply a remesh for cleaner geometry
5. **Offset** repositions the extrusion relative to the mesh line
6. Enable **custom cost** and set **cost per mm** to estimate material costs
7. Toggle **disp cost** to display the cost value in the viewport
8. The **ext size** and **cost** outputs provide the extrusion dimensions and calculated cost

Alternatively, use **t-slot profiles** as a standalone node group to generate just the cross-section profile without extrusion along a path.

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

## Tarot Stipple Material

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: MATERIAL | Blender: 4.5+ | Tags: MoGraph, Well Tarot, Design, Visualization, Digital Art, Generative | Handle: tarot-stipple-material

# Tarot Stipple Material

## Description

The custom stipple-y Tarot Stipple Material I produced to quickly make the thumbnails procedurally for this very website. It's a procedurally generated Geometry Nodes material that creates a distinctive stipple texture effect with outline control. Applied via Geometry Nodes, it separates geometry into fill, outline, and combined outputs for compositing flexibility.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ TAROT TOTEM MATERIAL SETTINGS            │
├──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                               Geometry   ○
│                                outline   ○
│                                   fill   ○
│                                          │
○ Geometry                                 │
□ Selection                                │
◆ Material                                 │
◉ color                                    │
▮ brightness                               │
▮ distort scale                            │
▮ stipple size                             │
▮ outline                                  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Tarot Stipple Material** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. Connect **Geometry** to the mesh you want to apply the stipple effect to
3. Use **Selection** to limit the effect to specific faces
4. Assign a base **Material** for the stipple shader
5. Set **color** for the stipple tint
6. **brightness** controls the overall luminance of the stipple pattern
7. **distort scale** adjusts the procedural noise distortion applied to the stipple dots
8. **stipple size** controls the density/size of the stipple dots
9. **outline** adjusts the thickness of the edge outline effect
10. Use the separate **outline** and **fill** geometry outputs for compositing different passes

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

### Usage Guide

# Tarot Stipple Material - Asset User Guide

## Getting Started

### Installation

1. **Download the Asset**
   - Locate the Tarot Stipple Material `.blend` file in your downloads or asset library.
   - Ensure you have Blender 4.5 or later installed.

### Adding Tarot Stipple Material to a New Asset Library

To create a dedicated library for NO3D assets:

1. **Create a New Asset Library Folder**
   - In your file system, create a new folder and name it **no3d tools library** (or a name you'll recognize).
2. **Add Tarot Stipple Material**
   - Copy `Tarot Stipple Material.blend` into your new **no3d tools library** folder.

3. **Register the Library in Blender**
   - In Blender, go to **Edit > Preferences > File Paths > Asset Libraries**.
   - Click **Add**, then select your new **no3d tools library** folder. Click **Accept** and close Preferences.

Now, in Blender's Asset Browser, choose **no3d tools library** from the Asset Libraries dropdown. Your Tarot Stipple Material assets are ready to use—just drag them into your project!

---

### Adding Tarot Stipple Material to an Existing Asset Library

If you already have an asset library folder (for example, **no3d tools library**):

1. Copy `Tarot Stipple Material.blend` into your existing **no3d tools library** folder.
2. Open `Tarot Stipple Material.blend` in Blender.
3. Mark your desired assets (**Mark as Asset**), and save the file.
4. In Blender's Asset Browser, make sure your asset library is selected. Tarot Stipple Material assets will now appear—drag them into your project whenever you need them!

That's it!

---

## Using the Tarot Stipple Material Asset

### Drag-and-Drop from Asset Browser

1. **Open Asset Browser**
   - Go to the Asset Browser panel in Blender (accessible in the main window, or via Area change).

2. **Find Tarot Stipple Material**
   - Look in the Materials section of your asset library where you saved the Tarot Stipple Material asset.

3. **Apply the Material**
   - Select your object in the 3D viewport.
   - Drag and drop Tarot Stipple Material from the Asset Browser onto your selected object.
   - The material will be applied immediately and you'll see the stipple texture in the viewport.

---

## Customizing the Tarot Stipple Material Asset

Tarot Stipple Material is fully customizable through Blender's Material Properties panel.

### Material Parameters

When the material is applied, you can adjust these parameters in the Material Properties panel:

- **Stipple Density**: Controls how many stipple dots appear per unit area. Higher values create more dense patterns.
- **Stipple Scale**: Adjusts the size of individual stipple marks. Larger values create bigger dots.
- **Contrast**: Fine-tunes the visibility and sharpness of the stipple pattern. Higher contrast makes dots more distinct.
- **Base Color**: The underlying color of the material surface.
- **Stipple Color**: The color of the stipple dots themselves (can differ from base color for layered effects).

### For Materials

- Imported Tarot Stipple Material comes fully set up with optimized node groups.
- To tweak how the stipple effect appears, check the Material Properties panel for node group controls.
- You can modify individual nodes in the Shader Editor for advanced customization.

---

## Advanced Usage

- **Mixing with Other Materials**: Combine Tarot Stipple Material with Blender's other materials using Mix Shader nodes to create advanced layered effects.

- **Procedural Customization**: Modify the node group structure in the Shader Editor to add new parameters, dynamic properties, or animations for unique, dynamic stipple effects.

- **Texture Mapping**: Use UV mapping or procedural coordinates to control how the stipple pattern is applied across your object's surface.

- **Animation**: Keyframe material properties to create animated stipple effects that evolve over time.

---

## Troubleshooting Common Material Issues

- **Material Not Showing Up in Asset Browser**: 
  - Ensure your Tarot Stipple Material is saved and marked as an Asset (`Right-click → Mark as Asset`) in Blender.
  - Check that your Asset Library path is set up in Blender Preferences ([Blender Manual: Asset Libraries](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/editors/asset_browser.html#asset-libraries)).

- **Material Parameters Not Editable**:
  - If you can't see or adjust parameters, confirm the material's node group inputs are properly exposed ([Blender: Exposing Group Inputs](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/modeling/geometry_nodes/geometry_node_group.html#inputs)).
  - In the Material Properties panel, make sure any custom properties or controls are visible; unhide them in the node group if needed.

- **Unexpected Results or Errors**:
  - Use the recommended Blender version (4.5+). Material nodes may not be fully compatible across versions.
  - Check for mismatched or missing node connections in the Shader Editor.

- **Slow or Laggy Viewport Performance**:
  - Complex material node setups can increase computational load. Try simplifying the material or reducing viewport quality settings.
  - Switch to the Eevee render engine for faster feedback ([Viewport Performance Tips](https://www.blender.org/manual/editors/3dview/navigate/viewports.html#viewport-performance)).
  - Use "Simplify" viewport settings for faster preview ([Blender Docs: Simplify](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/render/simplify.html)).

- **Stipple Pattern Not Visible**:
  - Ensure your object has proper UV mapping or generated coordinates.
  - Check that the material is assigned to the correct material slot.
  - Adjust lighting in your scene to make the stipple texture more visible.
  - Increase the Contrast parameter to make stipple dots more distinct.

- **Asset Updates Not Showing in Browser**:
  - After making changes, re-save and re-mark the material as an asset, then refresh the Asset Browser.
  - If problems persist, restart Blender or force a library reload ([Asset Browser Troubleshooting](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/editors/asset_browser.html#troubleshooting)).

> For additional support, see the [Blender Material Manual](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/render/materials/index.html) and [Asset Browser Documentation](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/editors/asset_browser.html), or search [Blender Stack Exchange](https://blender.stackexchange.com/questions/tagged/materials) for community solutions.

---

## Tips and Best Practices

1. **Start Simple**: Begin by applying the material with its default settings to see how it works out of the box. Once you're comfortable, experiment by tweaking parameters and making more advanced adjustments for custom results.

2. **Customize and Save**: Tweak Tarot Stipple Material and save your own variations to your Asset Library. For guidance on making your own custom materials, refer to [Blender's Material Documentation](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/render/materials/index.html).

3. **Lighting Considerations**: The stipple pattern is most visible with appropriate lighting. Use directional lights or area lights to enhance the texture's visibility.

4. **Color Harmony**: Experiment with complementary or analogous color schemes for the base and stipple colors to create harmonious tarot-inspired palettes.

---

## Support & Resources

- [Blender Asset Browser Documentation](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/editors/asset_browser.html)
- [Blender Material Manual](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/render/materials/index.html)
- [Blender Shader Editor Guide](https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/editors/shader_editor.html)

---

## Threaded Insert

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: OBJECT | Blender: 5.0+ | Tags: blender, geometry-nodes, 3d-printing, hardware, threaded-insert, mechanical | Handle: threaded-insert

# Threaded Insert

## Description

A parametric threaded insert (heat-set insert) generator for 3D printed parts. Set the diameter and get three outputs: the insert geometry itself, a boolean cutter for creating clean pockets, and a combined view of both.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ THREADED INSERT                      │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                            Insert    ○
│                              Bool    ○
│                               All    ○
│                                      │
○ Geometry                             │
▮ Diameter        ⌖                    │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **Threaded Insert** from the Asset Browser into your scene
2. Set **Diameter** to match your heat-set insert size (e.g., M3 = 4.0mm, M4 = 5.0mm)
3. The modifier outputs three geometry channels:
   - **Insert** — the insert body (for visualization)
   - **Bool** — a boolean cutter shape (subtract from your part to create the pocket)
   - **All** — combined insert + boolean geometry

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

```
◆ input.rot2                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## View Crv Points

Type: Blender Add-on | Asset: GEOMETRY | Blender: 5.0.0+ | Tags: blender, addon, 3d, asset | Handle: view-crv-points

# View Crv Points

## Description

A simple diagnostic utility that converts curve control points into visible point cloud geometry. Useful for debugging curve-based workflows where you need to see exactly where the control points are.

## Parameters

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ VIEW CRV PTS                         │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│                            Points    ○
│                                      │
○ Curve                                │
▮ Radius                   ⌖           │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

⌖ = gizmo-enabled (direct editing in 3D viewport)
```

## Usage

1. Drag **view crv pts** from the Asset Browser into your Geometry Nodes editor
2. Connect **Curve** to the curve whose control points you want to visualize
3. **Radius** controls the size of each point marker

## Changelog

### Version 1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core functionality

## Support

For questions or issues with this asset, please contact The Well Tarot.

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

```
▮ Blur/Relax surface iterations                │
▮ Strand Radius              ⌖                 │
▮ hi res                                       │
▮ Tigthen to Surface                           │
◉ color                                        │
```

## Voronoi Wrapper Scene

Type: Blender Scene | Asset: SCENE | Blender: 4.0.0+ | Tags: blender, scene, putty, product-design | Handle: voronoi-wrapper-scene

Complete Blender scene with voronoi putty wrapping tools, reference objects (AirPods Max, lighter, screwdriver), and a photo studio setup

## Parameters

```
Scene file containing multiple putty wrapper assets and a thumb photo studio.
Includes: Airpods Max, Bic Lighter, Driver Shank, Screwdriver, Spike Putty 1, Voronoi Putty 1, No3d Knurler node group.
```

## License

Personal/non-commercial use only. Email joe@welltarot.com for commercial licensing or custom utility development.

---

# Membership Addon Guide

# No3d Tools Membership — Install & Usage Guide

## What It Does

The No3d Tools Membership addon syncs your entire No3d Tools asset library directly into Blender. Once installed and activated with your license key, every asset — geometry node tools, materials, mesh objects — appears in Blender's Asset Browser, ready to drag into any project. New assets are synced automatically when you open Blender.

---

## Requirements

- Blender 4.5 or newer
- An active No3d Tools subscription ([subscribe here](https://no3dtools.com/subscribe.html))
- Your license key (sent to your email after subscribing)

---

## Installation

### Step 1: Download the Addon

Download the latest `no3d_tools_membership.zip` from the link in your license key email, or from your [account page](https://no3dtools.com/account.html).

### Step 2: Install in Blender

1. Open Blender
2. Go to **Edit → Preferences → Add-ons**
3. Click **Install...** (top right)
4. Navigate to the downloaded `no3d_tools_membership.zip`
5. Click **Install Add-on**

### Step 3: Enable the Addon

1. In the Add-ons list, search for **"No3d Tools Membership"**
2. Check the box to enable it
3. The addon preferences panel will expand

---

## Setup

### Enter Your License Key

1. In the addon preferences (Edit → Preferences → Add-ons → No3d Tools Membership), find the **License** section
2. Paste your license key into the **Key** field
   - Format: `NO3D-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX`
   - Your key was emailed to you after subscribing
3. Click **Validate**
4. Status should show: **Active** ✓

If you don't have your license key:
- Check your email for "No3d Tools — Your License Key"
- Visit [no3dtools.com/account.html](https://no3dtools.com/account.html) to look it up

### Set Your Library Path

The addon stores synced assets in a local folder. The default is `~/no3d-tools-library/`.

To change it:
1. In the addon preferences, find the **Library** section
2. Click the folder icon next to **Path**
3. Choose where you want your assets stored
4. The folder will be created automatically if it doesn't exist

### Sync Your Library

1. Click **Sync Now** in the addon preferences
2. The addon will:
   - Validate your license
   - Download the asset manifest
   - Download all .blend files that are new or updated
   - Skip files that haven't changed (checksum-based)
3. First sync downloads the entire library (~500MB depending on your plan)
4. Future syncs only download what's changed (usually <10MB)

### Enable Auto-Sync

Check **"Sync on startup"** to automatically sync when Blender opens. The sync runs in the background — it won't block you from working.

---

## Using Your Assets

### Open the Asset Browser

1. In any Blender workspace, open the **Asset Browser** (or change a panel to Asset Browser type)
2. In the dropdown at the top left, select **NO3D Tools**
3. All your synced assets will appear

### Using Assets in Your Scene

- **Geometry Node tools**: Drag onto any mesh object to add as a modifier
- **Materials**: Drag onto any object to apply
- **Mesh objects**: Drag into the viewport to add to your scene
- **Collections**: Drag to instance an entire collection

### Browsing by Category

Assets are organized by Blender's catalog system. Use the sidebar in the Asset Browser to filter by category (tools, materials, objects, etc.).

---

## Managing Your Subscription

### Check Status

In the addon preferences, the **Status** line shows:
- **Active** ✓ — Your subscription is current
- **Grace Period** ⚠ — Payment failed, you have 7 days to update your payment method
- **Expired** ✗ — Subscription ended, sync disabled

### Update Payment / Cancel

Visit [no3dtools.com/account.html](https://no3dtools.com/account.html) to:
- View your subscription status
- Update your payment method
- Cancel your subscription
- Access Stripe's billing portal

### Re-subscribe

If your subscription expired, visit [no3dtools.com/subscribe.html](https://no3dtools.com/subscribe.html) to re-subscribe. Your existing license key will be reactivated — you don't need a new one.

---

## Troubleshooting

### "Invalid license key"
- Make sure you copied the full key including the `NO3D-` prefix
- Check for extra spaces before or after the key
- Verify your subscription is active at [no3dtools.com/account.html](https://no3dtools.com/account.html)

### "Sync failed"
- Check your internet connection
- Try clicking **Validate** first, then **Sync Now**
- If the error persists, the server may be temporarily down — try again in a few minutes

### Assets not showing in Asset Browser
- Make sure the **NO3D Tools** library is selected in the Asset Browser dropdown (top left)
- Try closing and reopening the Asset Browser
- Check that the library path in addon preferences points to the correct folder
- Run **Sync Now** to refresh

### Addon not appearing in Preferences
- Make sure you installed the `.zip` file, not an extracted folder
- Restart Blender after installation
- Check that you're running Blender 4.5 or newer

### Lost your license key
- Check your email for "No3d Tools — Your License Key"
- Visit [no3dtools.com/account.html](https://no3dtools.com/account.html) and enter your email
- Contact support if you can't find it

---

## Quick Reference

| Action | How |
|--------|-----|
| Install addon | Edit → Preferences → Add-ons → Install → select .zip |
| Enter license key | Addon preferences → License → Key field → Validate |
| Sync assets | Addon preferences → Sync Now |
| Auto-sync | Addon preferences → check "Sync on startup" |
| Browse assets | Asset Browser → select "NO3D Tools" library |
| Use a tool | Drag from Asset Browser onto a mesh object |
| Manage subscription | [no3dtools.com/account.html](https://no3dtools.com/account.html) |
| Get support | [no3dtools.com](https://no3dtools.com) |
